ocr.text: In the analysis of Siemens AG's performance from 2019 to 2024, the Quality Metrics table shows a decreasing defect rate,
starting at 2.3% in 2019, with corresponding total revenue of $95M and net income of $10M, while CAPEX was $10M.
Year  QualityMetrics  TotalRevenue(USD)
DefectRate(%)  NetIncome(USD)  CAPEX(USD)
Trend  Decreasing  Increasing  Increasing
The defect rate improved to 2.5% in 2020 with total revenue of $100M and net income of $12M, and then decreased to 1.8% in
2021, reflecting total revenue of $110M and net income of $15M with CAPEX at $15M.
PredictiveMaintenanceMetrics  TotalSavings  UnitsMonitored  DowntimeReduction  CostofDowntime
Year  Savings(%)  UnitsSold  Headcount  NumberofBranches  MachineUtilization  MaintenanceCosts  TotalRevenue
In 2022, the defect rate further decreased to 1.5%, achieving total revenue of $120M and net income of $18M, with CAPEX at
$18M. The trend continued into 2023 with a defect rate of 1.2%, total revenue of $130M, and net income of $20M, along with
CAPEX at $20M, culminating in 2024 with the lowest defect rate of 1.0%, total revenue of $140M, and net income of $22M,
while CAPEX reached $22M. The Predictive Maintenance Metrics table reveals a significant upward trend in savings
percentage, starting at 15% in 2019 with total savings of $2M, 1500 units monitored, a headcount of $500K, number of
branches at 95%, machine utilization at 95%, maintenance costs of $1M, and total revenue at $20M. In 2020, savings increased
to 18%, with 1600 units sold, $1.8M in downtime reduction, a headcount of $450K, 96% machine utilization, maintenance
costs of $950K, and total revenue of $22M. The trend continued with 20% savings in 2021, 1700 units sold, $1.5M in downtime
reduction, a headcount of $400K, 97% machine utilization, maintenance costs of $900K, and total revenue of $24M. In 2022,
savings reached 25%, monitoring 1800 units, with $1.2M in downtime reduction, headcount of $350K, 98% machine
utilization, maintenance costs of $850K, and total revenue of $26M. By 2023, savings peaked at 30%, with 1900 units
monitored, $1M in downtime reduction, headcount of $300K, 99% machine utilization, maintenance costs of $800K, and total
revenue of $28M.
